note: FROZEN pre-registration (#976) — never edit; delete deliberately to regenerate.

predictions.claim_natural: During the first 7 days (Days 1–7), nightly time-in-bed will consistently reach the 7.5-hour target window defined by the 9:00 PM bedtime and 4:30 AM wake time, meaning the wearable will log at least 7.5 hours in bed on each of the 7 nights.

Over the first 14 days (Days 1–14), nightly HRV readings will trend upward toward the 50 ms biomarker target as the fixed 9:00 PM–4:30 AM sleep schedule establishes a consistent circadian anchor, with the Day 14 reading expected to be higher than the Day 1 reading.
Daily calorie log entries will show intake at or below the 1500 kcal target on at least 5 of the first 7 logged days, confirming early adherence to the 1500 kcal/day nutrition protocol.

Daily protein log entries will meet or exceed the 170 g protein floor on at least 10 of the first 14 logged days, confirming that the protein floor is being hit consistently within the 11:30am-7:30pm eating window.

During the first 14 days, daily walking will consistently meet the 3-mile minimum on all 5 post-work weekdays per week, as prescribed by the Foundation phase plan, confirming adherence to the daily movement requirement before any intensity escalation.

Adherence to the 11:30am–7:30pm eating window will be maintained on at least 6 out of 7 days during the first 7 days, as the structured constraint is novel and motivation is highest at the outset of a defined start date.

The 9:00 PM bedtime target will be logged as met on fewer days than the 4:30 AM wake target over the first 14 days, because socially-driven evening behavior is harder to curtail than a morning alarm, making bedtime the weaker of the two sleep-discipline anchors in the opening weeks.

Body weight will trend downward from the stated start weight of 300.8 lbs over the first 14 days, driven by the 1500 kcal/day caloric target combined with daily ~3-mile walking and 5 gym days/week; a measurable decline on the scale is expected by Day 14.

Daily protein intake will consistently meet or exceed the 170 g protein floor across the first 14 days as logged, given that 170 g protein alone accounts for 680 kcal of the 1500 kcal/day target, leaving a structurally constrained but achievable macro distribution that anchors protein compliance from Day 1.

Adherence to the 11:30am–7:30pm eating window (an 8-hour daily window) will be maintained on at least 6 out of 7 days per week across the first 14 days, as logged meal timestamps will consistently fall within that window, reflecting the metabolic benefit of time-restricted feeding beginning on Day 1.

Daily fiber intake logs will show the 25 g fiber minimum being met on at least 5 out of every 7 days within the first 14 days, as the 1500 kcal/day target combined with the stated 25 g fiber floor creates a high-fiber-density dietary structure that supports favorable postprandial glucose regulation from the outset of the plan.

Resting heart rate (RHR) will trend downward over the first 14 days, consistent with the plan's 55 bpm long-term target, as daily walking (~3 miles) and 150 min/week Zone 2 activity begin imposing a parasympathetic stimulus from Day 1.

HRV will trend upward over the first 14 days, moving toward the plan's 50 ms target, driven by the ramp-discipline protocol — reduced volume in Week 1, no failure sets, and no PRs before Day 21 — which limits acute physiological stress and supports autonomic recovery.

Over the first 14 days, daily calorie log entries will be present for all 14 days, establishing a complete N=1 baseline data series with zero missing observations — a necessary condition for any within-subject statistical inference on the 1500 kcal/day target.

Across the first 14 days, the within-subject day-to-day variance in logged body weight will be detectable (i.e., at least one reading will differ from the 300.8 lb start weight), confirming that the daily weigh-in time series has sufficient signal variability to support trend modeling before Day 21.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On days when logged calories are at or under the 1500 kcal plan target, next-day weight will be lower than after days over target.
hypotheses.evidence: Pre-registered at genesis from the plan itself: 1500 kcal/day target from a 300.8 lb start (config/user_goals.json); MacroFactor logs daily calories and Withings logs daily weight, so both arms are directly measurable.
hypotheses.confirmation_criteria: Mean next-day weight at least 0.1 lbs lower on adherent days within 30 days, 95% CI excluding 0.
hypotheses.actionable_if_confirmed: Hold the 1,500 kcal baseline through the Foundation phase; escalate only if the trend stalls.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On days reaching roughly 6,000+ steps (the 3-mile daily-movement floor), next-day Whoop recovery will be higher than after low-movement days.
hypotheses.evidence: Pre-registered at genesis from the plan itself: daily movement target '3 miles walking minimum (steps will vary by stride length, roughly 6,000-7,000 steps)' (config/user_goals.json); Apple Health logs steps and Whoop logs recovery daily.
hypotheses.confirmation_criteria: Next-day recovery at least 3 points higher on 6,000+ step days within 30 days, 95% CI excluding 0.
hypotheses.actionable_if_confirmed: Protect the daily walk as a recovery lever, not just an energy-expenditure line.
